symptoms.symptom_name: Episodic cutaneous flushing of face, neck and upper chest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Episodic vasomotor flushing is a cardinal feature of carcinoid syndrome, especially when recurrent and alcohol-triggered.
Flushing occurs in anaphylaxis too, but the recurrent episodic pattern without urticaria points away from it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse secretory watery diarrhoea with cramping

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secretory diarrhoea driven by serotonin and other mediators is a core component of the carcinoid tetrad.
Watery diarrhoea is non-specific, but here it co-occurs with vasomotor and respiratory features.
Bronchospasm from mediator release is part of the carcinoid tetrad, though less common than flushing/diarrhoea.
Acute bronchospasm is a red-flag airway feature that mandates excluding anaphylaxis and drives escalation.
Wheeze fits asthma, but asthma cannot explain the flushing, diarrhoea or right-heart signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alcohol as an acute symptom trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alcohol is a classic provocateur of carcinoid mediator release and flushing.
Alcohol is also a recognised trigger of mast cell mediator release.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tricuspid regurgitation murmur with elevated JVP (carcinoid heart disease)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Right-sided (tricuspid/pulmonary) valvular disease from serotonin-driven plaque is highly characteristic of chronic carcinoid syndrome; a clinician auscultation finding, sealed.
Chronic flushing leads to facial telangiectasia — a marker of longstanding carcinoid syndrome; a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss and recurrent prior episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss with recurrent episodic vasomotor symptoms points to an underlying neuroendocrine tumour.
Elevated urinary 5-HIAA is the biochemical hallmark of serotonin-secreting carcinoid; never available via telehealth in real time — sealed answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of urticaria/angioedema, allergen exposure or new foods/medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No allergen, no urticaria/angioedema and a recurrent episodic pattern argue against true IgE-mediated anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia with fluctuating blood pressure (haemodynamic instability)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vasomotor instability with tachycardia reflects mediator surge and carcinoid-crisis risk; the measured vitals are clinician-obtained and sealed. The patient can report a racing-heart sensation, not a measured BP.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Episodic flushing + secretory diarrhoea + bronchospasm + elevated urinary 5-HIAA together are highly specific for carcinoid syndrome.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Carcinoid syndrome with carcinoid heart disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tricuspid regurgitation with raised JVP plus facial telangiectasia indicate chronic, established mediator exposure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess dehydration and electrolyte loss from high-volume secretory diarrhoea — a hospital blood test, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum mast-cell tryptase (acute) to help exclude anaphylaxis/mastocytosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Helps discriminate anaphylaxis/mast cell activation from a carcinoid vasomotor surge; drawn in hospital.
Biochemical confirmation of serotonin-secreting carcinoid; a specialist investigation, not real-time.
Neuroendocrine tumour marker supporting the diagnosis.
Assess tricuspid/pulmonary valve involvement and right-heart function (carcinoid heart disease).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cross-sectional imaging + Ga-68 DOTATATE PET for tumour localisation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Specialist localisation of the primary tumour and metastases once the syndrome is confirmed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad allergy/food-panel testing in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acute allergy panels do not guide emergency management here and would distract from stabilisation and the correct workup.
